opp.name: IntuSurg_June 26'
opp.next_step: June 15 Rick: Direct Procurement deal, already has Coupa for P2P, Jaggeur for CLM Seeking direct procurement case study Need SC for qualifying us out, so I proposed a Discovery. Wants us to tie into SAP’s suppliers and Jaggeur for contracts Using SAP for Direct Proc today, but wants AI for Direct – need to get advanced.
opp.ais_why:
• Intuitive Surgical is 'seeking automation for direct procurement, supplier collaboration, and exception handling above SAP,' with an 'executive champion confirmed with budget urgency.'
• ANA, Agentic AI and Merlin are scoped, aligning the automation ask with agentic capability.
• The Agentic-platform-discussed flag is not yet set and the immediate step is a discovery session to pinpoint pains, so the automation appetite is clear but AI-specific enthusiasm is still to be confirmed.

items.quote: Rick asked Siva on 2026-06-12: 'Are you the executive champion for an initiative like this within Intuitive?' No affirmative answer captured. Siva's title (Sr Supply Chain Digital Product Manager) is technical/project level, not executive/budget authority.
No champion identified. Siva is neutral/exploratory, not advocating. Zero OpportunityContactRoles. No senior stakeholder introduction or internal advocacy evidence.
No buyer-stated evaluation plan, timeline, or approval gates. No senior exec or cross-functional committee engaged.
Operational pain articulated (manual renewals, tracking gaps, automation needs) but no quantified cost, savings, cycle-time, or headcount impact stated.
Buyer requires Zycus to tie into SAP suppliers and Jaggaer contracts (Next Step 2026-06-15). Technical feasibility and integration architecture not yet scoped or proven.
Single-threaded: only Siva Paturi engaged across four touchpoints. Zero OpportunityContactRoles. No IT, procurement leadership, finance, or executive sponsor mapped.
items.source: Prefetch: 0 buyer-side contact roles; Avoma 2026-04-27 to 2026-06-12

sources: Avoma call 2026-06-12, Siva Paturi (buyer): supplier base ~500, scaling objective, manual renewals, tracking gaps, automation needs
Next Step 2026-06-15: 'Using SAP for Direct Proc today, but wants AI for Direct'
metrics.narrative: Buyer articulated operational pain on 2026-06-12 call: scaling supplier base (currently ~500 suppliers), manual contract renewals, difficulty tracking expired/expiring suppliers, and desire for high automation in supplier communications (autonomous shipping updates, pull-in requests, exception handling). No quantified ROI, cost savings, cycle-time reduction, or headcount impact stated. Buyer expressed interest in seeing relevance ('let's connect and see what you have and if it is relevant for us') but has not committed to a value-case workshop or ROI build. The lack of quantified metrics leaves the business case qualitative and unanchored to a CFO-grade justification, which will be required at vendor-selection and EB-approval stages. Making metrics concrete is a necessary move before commercials.

sources: Avoma calls 2026-04-27 to 2026-06-12: Siva Paturi present on all four; no senior exec joined
Avoma call 2026-06-12: Rick asked Siva if he is the executive champion; no clear affirmative
OpportunityContactRole: 0 roles on opportunity; no Champion role assigned
champion.narrative: No champion identified. Siva Paturi is the sole engaged contact across all four touchpoints (2026-04-27 to 2026-06-12) and is a technical/project-level influencer (Sr Supply Chain Digital Product Manager), not a power sponsor. Rick asked Siva on 2026-06-12 if he is the executive champion; no affirmative answer captured. Siva's engagement is neutral/exploratory ('let's connect and see if it is relevant for us') rather than advocacy. He has not introduced Zycus to senior stakeholders, driven the agenda, or pushed back on competitors. Allison Silveus (Supply Chain Training Program Manager) appeared on two early calls (2026-04-27, 2026-05-11) but is also a non-executive role and has not re-engaged since. No OpportunityContactRole of Champion, and no evidence of internal advocacy. Single-threaded, no champion, no EB access—deal is at high risk of stalling or being controlled by a competitor who multi-threads faster. Building a champion is the second-highest priority after EB mapping.

sources: Next Step 2026-06-15: 'Using SAP for Direct Proc today, but wants AI for Direct – need to get advanced'
Avoma call 2026-06-12, Siva: 'We're not looking for any indirect solution at this point' (Coupa/Jaggaer out of scope)
Next Step 2026-06-15: 'Wants us to tie into SAP's suppliers and Jaggeur for contracts' (integration requirement)
competition.narrative: SAP is the incumbent for Direct Procurement (Next Step 2026-06-15: 'Using SAP for Direct Proc today, but wants AI for Direct'). Buyer is dissatisfied with SAP's AI capability for Direct materials and is exploring Zycus as an AI-driven alternative. However, replacing an incumbent ERP-adjacent system (SAP) carries high switching cost, integration risk, and political inertia. No evidence that SAP has been formally disqualified or that Intuitive is running a competitive RFP with multiple vendors. Coupa (P2P) and Jaggaer (CLM) are in the stack but stated as out-of-scope ('not looking for any indirect solution', 2026-06-12 call); they are not direct competitors for this Direct Procurement use case. The wedge is Merlin/Agentic AI for Direct materials sourcing and autonomous supplier communications, areas where SAP is weak. However, the buyer has not confirmed Zycus can integrate with SAP supplier data and Jaggaer contracts—integration feasibility is the hurdle that will determine if Zycus can displace SAP or if the project stalls on technical blockers.

sources: Avoma call 2026-06-12, Siva: manual contract renewals, tracking gaps for expired/expiring suppliers, desire for high automation in supplier interactions
Avoma call 2026-06-12, Siva: current supplier base ~500, scaling objective
Next Step 2026-06-15: 'Using SAP for Direct Proc today, but wants AI for Direct'
identify_pain.narrative: Buyer articulated clear operational pain on 2026-06-12 call: manual contract renewals, inability to track expired/expiring supplier agreements, and lack of automation in supplier communications (shipping updates, pull-in requests, exception handling). Siva stated the current supplier base is ~500 and scaling is an objective, implying the manual processes do not scale. The buyer also stated they use SAP for Direct Procurement today but want AI capability, indicating dissatisfaction with SAP's AI/automation for Direct materials. Pain owner is Siva Paturi (Sr Supply Chain Digital Product Manager), a technical/operational role. However, no senior exec (CPO, VP Supply Chain) has confirmed this pain as a strategic priority, and no financial impact (cost of manual work, error rate, delayed renewals) has been quantified. Pain is real and operational but not yet elevated to executive/strategic level with a CFO-grade cost-of-inaction case.

sources: Next Step 2026-06-15: Jaggaer CLM integration required (contract-execution touchpoint)
No other contracting, legal, or procurement discussion in available evidence
paper_process.narrative: Contracting, legal, procurement, and InfoSec processes are unknown. No MSA, SOW, security review, or procurement-channel discussion has occurred. The Jaggaer CLM integration requirement (Next Step 2026-06-15) implies that contract execution may flow through Jaggaer, but mechanics are unconfirmed. No SI/partner involvement mentioned. No Current_Contract_Expiration__c date to create a forcing function. At Qualified stage, paper-process clarity is not expected, but it will become critical at vendor-selection and negotiation. The Discovery and subsequent deeper engagement must surface InfoSec requirements, legal timeline, and procurement-approval steps to validate the November close date.

sources: Avoma call 2026-06-12: Rick asked Siva if he is the executive champion; no affirmative answer in available transcript
Prefetch: Siva Paturi title 'Sr Supply Chain Digital Product Manager' (technical/project role, not executive/budget authority)
OpportunityContactRole query: 0 roles on this opportunity
economic_buyer.narrative: Economic Buyer is unmapped. On the 2026-06-12 call, Rick asked Siva Paturi: 'Are you the executive champion for an initiative like this within Intuitive?' No response captured in transcript excerpt. Siva's title (Sr Supply Chain Digital Product Manager per prefetch) suggests an influencer/technical evaluator role, not budget authority. No CFO, CPO, or senior exec (VP+ Procurement/Supply Chain) has appeared on any of the four touchpoints. The opportunity has zero contact roles, and no Salesforce field names a budget approver. Without EB access, commercials and ROI approval are speculative. Mapping the Economic Buyer is the single highest-priority gap before advancing past Discovery.

sources: Call sequence 2026-04-27 to 2026-06-12: only Siva Paturi and Allison Silveus (project/training roles) engaged; no senior exec or procurement leadership
Next Step 2026-06-15: Discovery proposed by Rick but not scheduled; no buyer-stated timeline or evaluation milestones
decision_process.narrative: Decision process is unknown. No multi-stage evaluation plan, approval chain, or timeline has been stated by the buyer. The call sequence shows only technical/project-level engagement (Siva Paturi, Allison Silveus across 4 touchpoints 2026-04-27 to 2026-06-12); no senior exec, procurement lead, or cross-functional committee has joined. Rick proposed a Discovery session on 2026-06-15 but no scheduling confirmation, suggesting the buyer has not committed to a formal evaluation track. Salesforce fields (What_is_the_decision_process__c, Mandate__c, Purchase_approvals_Required_from__c) are not available in this org or were not queried. The lack of a defined process and the absence of senior stakeholders means Zycus is in exploratory conversation, not a structured RFP/vendor-selection process. Mapping the decision process and approval gates is a foundational Discovery objective.

sources: Avoma call 2026-06-12, Siva: 'We're not looking for any indirect solution at this point'
Next Step 2026-06-15: 'Wants us to tie into SAP's suppliers and Jaggeur for contracts; Using SAP for Direct Proc today, but wants AI for Direct'
Avoma call 2026-06-12: automation needs (autonomous communications for shipping, pull-ins, exceptions)
decision_criteria.narrative: Buyer stated Direct Procurement capability as the primary criterion on 2026-06-12 call and in Next Step (2026-06-15): must prove AI-driven Direct materials sourcing, not indirect. Integration with SAP (supplier data) and Jaggaer (CLM) is explicit requirement. Buyer also emphasized high automation (autonomous supplier communications). These are confirmed technical/functional criteria. However, the formal RFI/RFP criteria, decision scorecard, and evaluation committee structure are unknown. No BRD, shortlist rubric, or multi-vendor bake-off process has been surfaced. Criteria status is partial: core technical requirements are clear, but the full decision framework and relative weighting (cost vs AI vs integration vs ease-of-change) are unconfirmed. Surfacing the formal evaluation process and criteria during Discovery is critical.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. We're behind on this one — the reads below say why and what would change it.

ai_fit_signal.summary: Buyer explicitly stated desire for AI-driven Direct Procurement on 2026-06-15 ('wants AI for Direct – need to get advanced') and articulated high-automation goals (autonomous supplier communications) on 2026-06-12 call. No AIS score in Salesforce, but buyer behavior signals AI Curious to AI Hungry: they are exploring AI as a solution to operational pain (manual renewals, tracking, scaling) and are dissatisfied with SAP's AI capability. Zycus must convert this curiosity into conviction by demonstrating Merlin/Agentic AI superiority in Discovery.

items.change: Opportunity created and stage set to Qualified

Rick proposed Discovery session to prove Direct Procurement fit and address buyer concern about being qualified out (Next Step update)

deal_movement.summary: Deal created and qualified on 2026-06-12, same day as fourth buyer touchpoint. Last buyer engagement 2026-06-12 (19 days ago). Rep proposed Discovery on 2026-06-15 per Next Step but no scheduling confirmation or follow-up from buyer. No stage advancement since creation. Qualified stage for 19 days with no next-milestone activity is slow for a November close.

Deal is at high risk of stalling without a champion who has EB access and internal influence.

items.expected_effect: Restart deal momentum and create the forum to prove Direct Procurement fit, demonstrate SAP/Jaggaer integration feasibility, and map additional stakeholders. Without Discovery, deal stalls and November close becomes impossible.

items.action: Deliver Direct Procurement case study to Siva before Discovery session.
items.trigger: Buyer explicitly requested Direct Procurement case study on 2026-06-15 (Next Step: 'Seeking direct procurement case study'). Buyer concern: risk of being qualified out if Zycus cannot prove Direct materials capability.
items.expected_effect: Prove Direct Procurement credibility before Discovery, reduce buyer's qualification-out risk, and set stage for deeper technical engagement. Case study must show Direct materials sourcing AI, ideally in medical device or precision manufacturing vertical.

items.action: Request Siva introduce Zycus to VP/Director Procurement or Supply Chain during Discovery scheduling conversation.
items.trigger: Siva is a technical influencer (Sr Digital PM), not a power sponsor. Rick asked on 2026-06-12 if Siva is the executive champion; no affirmative answer. Zero OpportunityContactRoles and no senior stakeholder engagement across four touchpoints.
items.expected_effect: Multi-thread to Economic Buyer or decision authority. Siva cannot approve budget or drive decision; EB access is required to advance past Discovery and validate commercial viability.

items.action: Prepare SAP and Jaggaer integration architecture diagram and data-flow proof points for Discovery.
items.trigger: Buyer requires Zycus to tie into SAP suppliers and Jaggaer CLM (Next Step 2026-06-15). Integration feasibility is unproven and is a potential disqualifier.
items.expected_effect: De-risk integration concern in Discovery by showing proven SAP and Jaggaer connectors, reference architectures, and API/data-flow clarity. Integration doubt will kill the deal; proactive proof builds confidence.

items.action: Co-develop a mutual evaluation plan with Siva during Discovery, naming milestones and stakeholders for RFI/shortlist stages.
items.trigger: No decision process, timeline, or evaluation plan stated by buyer. Discovery proposed 2026-06-15 but no formal evaluation track confirmed. November close requires compressed enterprise motion (RFI, shortlist, vendor selection, commercials, contracting in ~4.5 months).
items.expected_effect: Convert exploratory interest into a structured evaluation with named gates, dates, and stakeholders. Mutual plan makes the November close credible and surfaces the Economic Buyer and decision committee.

confidence_signals.summary: No confidence signals. Buyer engagement is exploratory/neutral. No senior exec involvement, no champion, no EB mapped, no formal evaluation plan, no quantified ROI, and Discovery not yet scheduled despite 16 days since proposal. Deal is in discovery phase with no commitment signals.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 2026-06-12, qualified same day, 19 days in Qualified stage. 146 days to close (2026-11-24). Enterprise motion from Qualified to close typically requires 12-15 months; achieving close in ~5 months requires immediate Discovery execution, rapid proof-of-fit, and compressed evaluation. Last buyer touchpoint 2026-06-12 (19 days ago); rep proposed Discovery 2026-06-15 but no follow-up scheduled. At current pace (19 days with no gate advancement), November close is not credible.
evidence: Rep proposed Discovery on 2026-06-15 per Next Step to address buyer concern about being qualified out prematurely; no confirmation or scheduled date from buyer as of 2026-07-01 (16 days elapsed).

Buyer stated on 2026-06-12 call: 'We're not looking for any indirect solution at this point' and is seeking Direct Procurement proof (case study + Discovery). Zycus must prove fit or be disqualified early.
Integration requirement: buyer wants Zycus to tie into SAP suppliers and Jaggaer CLM (Next Step 2026-06-15). Technical feasibility unproven; integration complexity could delay or block advancement.

Single-threaded: only Siva Paturi engaged across 4 touchpoints (2026-04-27, 2026-05-11, 2026-06-09, 2026-06-12). No Economic Buyer, Decision Maker, or champion mapped. No contact roles on opportunity.
Pipeline forecast and Qualified stage are honest to current state but do not support a November close without immediate multi-gate compression.
north_star_verdict.headline: Discovery proposed 2026-06-15 but not yet scheduled as of 2026-07-01; deal must move from Qualified through multiple enterprise gates (RFI/RFP, shortlist, vendor selection, commercials, contracting) in 146 days while proving Direct Procurement fit and SAP/Jaggaer integration feasibility.
north_star_verdict.recommended_forecast: Pipeline (accurate; deal is genuinely early and unproven)

flags: Single-threaded: only one buyer stakeholder referenced, no multi-threading visible
No Avoma buyer calls discovered (pre-call stage, but Discovery must happen soon)
No quantified business case or metrics yet (expected gap at this early stage, Discovery will address)

Build a champion from current engagement. Siva is neutral/exploratory; convert him to advocate by demonstrating how Zycus AI solves his operational pain (manual renewals, tracking, automation) and positioning him as the internal AI transformation lead. A champion who introduces us to the EB and drives the agenda is the only path forward in a single-threaded deal.
best_practice_check.summary: Deal is single-threaded with no Economic Buyer or champion, and Discovery proposed 16 days ago with no buyer confirmation. To win, Zycus must multi-thread to senior procurement/supply-chain leadership, prove Direct Procurement fit and SAP/Jaggaer integration feasibility in Discovery, and build a champion who can navigate the approval process.

competitive_position.summary: SAP is the incumbent and the sole identified competitor, currently used for Direct Procurement. Buyer is dissatisfied with SAP's AI capability and is exploring Zycus as an AI-driven alternative (Next Step 2026-06-15: 'wants AI for Direct – need to get advanced'). SAP's threat level is high because it is entrenched (switching cost, integration, political inertia) and Intuitive has not stated SAP is being replaced—only that they want AI augmentation. Coupa and Jaggaer are in the stack but out-of-scope for this Direct Procurement deal. No other vendors (Ariba, GEP, Ivalua, Zip) mentioned. Zycus must prove Direct Procurement fit, SAP/Jaggaer integration feasibility, and AI superiority in Discovery to displace or overlay SAP. If integration is too complex or Direct Procurement proof is weak, buyer may default to SAP's roadmap rather than switching.

competitors.how_we_win: Lead with Merlin Agentic AI for Direct materials sourcing and autonomous supplier communications (pull-ins, renewals, exceptions). Prove seamless integration with SAP supplier data and Jaggaer CLM in Discovery. Position SAP as legacy/rule-based vs Zycus AI-native. Make Direct Procurement case study and live AI demo the centerpiece of Discovery to overcome buyer's risk of being 'qualified out'.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AI positioning is embryonic. Buyer explicitly wants AI for Direct Procurement ('wants AI for Direct – need to get advanced', Next Step 2026-06-15), and Zycus products include Merlin and Agentic AI. However, buyer has not seen a demo, case study, or proof of AI superiority over SAP. AI appetite is present but Zycus AI capability is unproven. Discovery is the make-or-break moment to position Merlin/Agentic AI as the Direct Procurement AI leader.

Integration feasibility is the critical unknown and a potential disqualifier.

business_case.evidence: Operational pain articulated on 2026-06-12: manual contract renewals, tracking gaps for expired/expiring suppliers, desire for high automation, scaling supplier base (~500 today). No quantified ROI, cost savings, cycle-time reduction, or headcount impact.

gaps: Three of four touchpoints have no recording/transcript; only 2026-06-12 call available for deep read. Earlier calls (2026-04-27, 2026-05-11, 2026-06-09) may contain stakeholder, competitive, or decision-process intel not captured.

No SOQL access to MEDDPICC 2.0 custom fields or extended synthesis-map fields (e.g., Business_Objectives__c, Decision_Maker_Identified__c, What_is_the_decision_process__c) in this sweep; relied on Next Step and single transcribed call for MEDDPICC reconstruction.
Zero OpportunityContactRoles on this opportunity; stakeholder map built from Avoma attendees and prefetch account contacts only. Cannot confirm roles (Champion, EB, DM) from Salesforce.
No OpportunityLineItem data queried; product scope taken from Products__c field only.
No field history for Amount, CloseDate, or Stage slips; deal created 2026-06-12 and has not moved, so no historical slip pattern available.

Buyer wants AI for Direct but has not confirmed Zycus superiority.
